content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
-US J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Mike Reynolds. Director 
Joshua Basin Water District 
P.O. Box 675 
61750 Chollita Road 
Joshua Tree. California 92252 
RE: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-11-014 
Dear Mr. Reynolds: 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 3:22-0886 
Febmary 15. 2010 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter is based on the facts presented; 
the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when 
it renders assistance. (lri re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) 
Additionally. our advice is limited to obligations arising under the Act. We do not 
address the applicability, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of 
interest or Government Code Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
Do you have a conflict of interest in submitting a proposal to the Mojave Water Agency 
to provide 400 low-flow water efficient toilets at a one day "event" that will distribute these 
toilets to residents within the Joshua Basin Water District? 
CONCLUSION 
No. 
1 The PolitiL-JI Reform Act is con1ained in Government Code Sections 8 IOOO through 91014. All statutory 
reforences are to the Govc:rnment Code. unless otherwiSI! indicated. The regulations of the Fair Politkal Practh.:es 
Cl1mmissilln are contained in Si:ctioos IX I IO through 18997 of Title:? of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatury referem:es are to Title:?.. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless othen\ise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No.A-11-014 
Page No. 2 
You were elected as a director to the board of the Joshua Basin Water District (the 
"District") in November 2008. The board is the governing body of the District pursuant to 
Section 30575 of the California Water Code and various provisions of the Government Code. 
You have been a building contractor and a plumbing contractor for about 30 years with the past 
11 years spent in Joshua Tree. Your business includes household plumbing maintenance such as 
repairing broken pipes, installing water heaters, and installing or replacing faucet, sink, and toilet 
fixtures. 
The District is a retail water purveyor covering about 100 miles with about 4,500 
customers. The source of water for the District is groundwater, of which there is an ample 
supply. The District uses about 1,500 acre feet of water annually. The District basins have about 
625,000 acre feet of water. The District has almost no natural recharge from either surface 
sources or precipitation. Due to this fact, in the future the District expects to replenish its aquifer 
from the State Water Project (Northern California) through the Mojave Water Agency (the 
"MW A"), although it has not purchased any water from MW A in the past. 
MW A is one of 29 water contractors with access to the State Water Project. They are a 
wholesale water agency that purveys water from Northern California, making it available to 
retail water agencies within 4,500 square miles of the High Desert of San Bernardino County, 
which includes the District. MW A, in cooperation with other water districts, is responsible for 
managing the region's water resources to insure a sustainable supply of water for present and 
future use. 
The District and MWA each has its own elected board of directors and they are entirely 
independent of one another. In the early 1990s, MW A constructed a 70 mile pipeline to the 
Morango Basin to make water available to the District and three other water districts Although 
the pipeline is owned and operated by MW A, the pipeline was paid for by the local water 
districts and the property owners within those districts after voting in favor of the pipeline and 
related tax. 
In the past, the District paid to extend the pipeline two miles to reach the District 
boundaries. The District is now planning to extend the pipeline an additional four miles to be 
able to purchase water and percolate it through the aquifer for future use. The District has 
purchased a site for the ponds and has nearly completed engineering for the ponds and the four 
mile pipeline. 
MW A has contributed $350,000 to the District and has pledged as additional $650.000 
toward the pond and pipeline ex.tension program. MW A has also applied to the State of 
California, on behalf of the District for Proposition 84 bonds funds for construction of the 
project. MWA has been designated by the State of California as the agency eligible to apply for 
the grant. The District would not qualify to submit a proposal for these Proposition 84 funds on 
its own. 


File No.A-11-014 
Page No. 3 
No action by the District board has been required during the past year relative to the 
pond, the pipeline project, or the Proposition 84 grant proposal. Any action has been authorized 
solely by the general manager without specific direction of the board. 
You wish to submit a proposal by which you will receive a fixed amount from MWA for 
each of the 400 toilets you provide. You will purchase the 400 toilets from a private vendor and 
store them at your business. On the day of the "event," residents will come to your business to 
pick up their new toilet. You will arrange for the old toilets to be turned in within two weeks to 
assure that the new toilets have been installed. You will also offer to deliver and install the 
toilets for a fee that will be charged directly to the resident for those residents who want such 
service. 
The District will not be involved in the event in any way. There is no agreement between 
the District and MWA for this event, and the District is not involved in any promotion of the 
event. 
ANALYSIS 
POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST 
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their 
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the 
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) Section 87100 
prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her 
official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. 
The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether an 
official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation l 8700(b ).) The general rule, 
however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes a governmental 
decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of his or her 
financial interests. 
STEPS 1: ARE You A PuBLIC OFFICIAL? 
As a member of the District, you are a public official under the Act. (Section 82048.) 
Consequently, you may not make, participate in making, or otherwise use your official position to 
influence any decision that will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on any of 
your economic interests. 
I 
STEP 2: ARE You l\iL\KING, PARTICIPATING IN MAKING, OR INFLUENCING A GOVElt'IMENTAL 
DECISION? 
Because you are acting in your capacity as a private contractor and not as a member of 
the District board and the District board will not be participating in the event in any manner, you 
will not be making or participating in the making of a governmental decision. To determine 


File No.A-11-014 
Page No. 4 
whether or not you may be using your official position to influence a governmental decision, we 
look to Regulation 18702.3, which states as follows: 
"(a) With regard to a governmental decision which is within or before an official's 
agency or an agency appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of his or her 
agency, the official is attempting to use his or her official position to influence the 
decision if, for the purpose of influencing the decision, the official contacts, or 
appears before, or otherwise attempts to influence, any member, officer, employee or 
consultant of the agency. Attempts to influence include, but are not limited to, 
appearances or contacts by the official on behalf of a business entity, client, or 
customer. 
"(b) With regard to a governmental decision which is within or before an agency not 
covered by subsection (a), the official is attempting to use his or her official position 
to influence the decision if, for the purpose of influencing the decision, the official 
acts or purports to act on behalf of, or as the representative of, his or her agency to 
any member, officer, employee or consultant of an agency. Such actions include, but 
are not limited to the use of official stationery." 
According to your facts, the District and MWA are entirely independent of one another. 
Accordingly, subdivision (b) above would control. Under that provision, so long as you do not 
act or purport to act on behalf of or as a representative of your agency, or use stationary or 
business cards or anything else that connects you with your agency, and your proposal is 
submitted solely in your limited capacity as the owner of your plumbing business, you will not 
be improperly influencing a governmental decision under the Act. 
Accordingly, you do not have a conflict of interest and our analysis ends at step two. If 
you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Scott Hallabrin 
G 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
WJL:jgl
